title: Midnight Bistro
description: An earthy and crunchy Japanese dish featuring a unique twist on hispi sweetheart cabbage cooked with tandoor spices for a midnight craving.
instructions: Wash and core the hispi sweetheart cabbage. Rinse thoroughly under cold water.
Slice the cabbage into quarters, then cut each quarter into thin, flat pieces.
In a large bowl, combine salt with coriander seeds and grind to a fine powder using mortar and pestle or a spice grinder.
Add tandoori powder, ginger paste, and sugar to the spice mix and stir until well combined.
Drizzle sesame oil over the cabbage slices. Sprinkle the spice mixture evenly on top of the cabbage pieces.
Gently toss the cabbage in the seasoned spice mixture for even coating.
Heat a cast iron skillet or shallow pan over medium-high heat. Once hot, add some vegetable or peanut oil.
Once the oil is hot and starts to shimmer, carefully place the coated cabbage slices into the skillet.
Cook for 5-7 minutes on each side until golden brown and crispy. Keep an eye out for burning.
Serve immediately as a snack with a side of spicy mayo or wasabi.
